title: Defining the Project Scope
concept: Scoping the task of classifying handwritten digits or images: target classes, dataset size, and accuracy goals.

exercises.prompt: What is an important first step before starting a project?
exercises.explain: Defining the scope helps clarify what we need to achieve in the project.

cards.title: What is Project Scope?
cards.body: Project scope outlines what you'll do in your project. It includes details like what you want to classify, the size of your dataset, and how accurate you want the results to be.

cards.title: Defining the Scope
cards.body: Defining the scope means setting clear boundaries for your project, like deciding which handwritten digits or images to classify.

cards.title: Project Scope is like a Map
cards.body: Just like a map outlines the routes you can take to reach your destination, project scope outlines the paths you can follow to complete your task.

cards.title: Example of Scoping
cards.body: For our handwritten digit project, your target classes might be digits 0-9, your dataset could be 60,000 images, and your accuracy goal might be 95%.

cards.title: Myth: You Don't Need to Plan
cards.body: Many think projects can succeed without planning. However, not defining scope often leads to confusion and missed objectives.

cards.title: Visualizing Project Scope
cards.body: Here’s a simple way to visualize project scope:

exercises.prompt: What is one of the goals when defining project scope for image classification?
exercises.explain: Deciding on the target classes is a key goal when defining the project scope, as it helps specify what you will classify.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Defining scope is just about dataset size.
exercises.explain: False. Defining scope also involves determining target classes and accuracy goals, not just the dataset size.

exercises.prompt: To improve project clarity, we need to define the _____ of our project.
exercises.sentence: To improve project clarity, we need to define the project scope of our project.
exercises.explain: The phrase 'project scope' comes from the sentence itself, emphasizing its importance in project clarity.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following is a reason for defining the project scope?

options.text: To ensure everyone agrees on the objectives
To take as long as needed

exercises.explain: Defining the project scope ensures that everyone involved understands and agrees on the project's objectives and limits.

exercises.prompt: In planning your image classification project, which components should you include in the project scope?

options.text: Target classes, dataset size, and accuracy goals

exercises.explain: The correct answer includes all essential components: target classes, dataset size, and accuracy goals to guide the project's direction.
